From the Hollywood Bowl and the Walt Disney Concert Hall down to the warehouses of the Arts District, this region runs on storytelling. Our wooden open-air photo booth was built for it.

Hollywood headlines mean rooftop premieres and after-parties along Sunset. Mid-Wilshire holds the museums — LACMA, the Petersen, the Academy Museum — where charity galas and product launches need a print station that fits the architecture, not against it. Down in DTLA, weddings happen at venues like Vibiana, the Millwick, and Hudson Loft; corporate events take over The Broad and the California Club. Every venue in the heart of the city wants the same thing: photography that holds up to the room.

We've covered events from the Greek Theatre after-parties to backyard weddings in Hancock Park, from Echo Park warehouse activations to Larchmont birthday dinners. The booth packs into two cases, sets up in 45 minutes, and reads as a piece of furniture — never as a kiosk. That's what makes it a good fit for an area that already has an aesthetic.
